questions.stem: In this schematic, three drugs bind the same membrane receptor: the middle molecule triggers a cellular 'Effect' while the rightmost molecule occupies the receptor but produces 'No effect.' What best explains the mechanism of the rightmost drug?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It binds and fully activates the receptor to trigger signaling

choices.label: B
choices.text: It reduces the receptor's baseline constitutive activity below normal

choices.label: C
choices.text: It partially activates the receptor to give a submaximal response

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is the endogenous ligand that normally activates the receptor

choices.label: E
choices.text: It binds the receptor without activating it, blocking agonist access

explanation.summary: An antagonist occupies the receptor and prevents agonist binding/activation, producing no downstream effect, in contrast to the agonist which activates signaling.
explanation.whyCorrect: An antagonist occupies the receptor and prevents agonist binding/activation, producing no downstream effect, in contrast to the agonist which activates signaling.
